title: Effect of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG Supplementation on Intestinal Permeability and Tight Junction Protein Expression in C57BL/6 Mice
summary: This study will evaluate whether 4 weeks of oral Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG supplementation reduces intestinal permeability by at least 30% in C57BL/6 mice compared to vehicle controls. Intestinal permeability will be assessed using FITC-dextran assay, and tight junction proteins claudin-1 and occludin will be quantified by Western blot and immunohistochemistry to determine the mechanism of action.

steps.title: Animal procurement and housing
steps.description: Obtain 40 male C57BL/6 mice, 6-8 weeks old, weight 20-25g. House in specific pathogen-free (SPF) conditions at 22±2°C, 50-60% humidity, 12h light/dark cycle. Provide standard chow and water ad libitum. Allow 7 days acclimatization before treatment initiation.

steps.title: Randomization and group assignment
steps.description: Randomly assign mice to two groups (n=20 per group): Group 1 (Control) receives vehicle (1% sterile skim milk in PBS), Group 2 (LGG) receives Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG at 1×10^9 CFU in 200μL vehicle. Use block randomization to ensure equal distribution.

steps.title: Preparation of L. rhamnosus GG suspension
steps.description: Culture L. rhamnosus GG (ATCC 53103) in MRS broth at 37°C anaerobically for 18-24h. Harvest by centrifugation at 3000×g for 10 min at 4°C. Wash twice with sterile PBS. Resuspend in 1% sterile skim milk in PBS to achieve 5×10^9 CFU/mL. Prepare fresh every 3 days and store at 4°C. Verify CFU by serial dilution plating on MRS agar.
steps.reference: Lebeer et al., Microbes and Infection. 2008

steps.title: Daily oral gavage administration
steps.description: Administer 200μL of either LGG suspension (1×10^9 CFU) or vehicle control via oral gavage once daily for 28 consecutive days. Use sterile gavage needles (20G, 38mm). Record body weight every 3 days and monitor for adverse effects. Administer treatments at the same time each day (9:00-11:00 AM).

steps.title: FITC-dextran permeability assay
steps.description: On day 29, fast mice for 4h with water ad libitum. Administer FITC-dextran (FD4, 4kDa, 600 mg/kg body weight) via oral gavage in PBS. After 4h, anesthetize mice with isoflurane and collect blood via cardiac puncture into heparinized tubes. Centrifuge at 3000×g for 15 min at 4°C to obtain plasma. Measure FITC fluorescence using fluorometer (excitation 485nm, emission 528nm). Calculate serum FITC-dextran concentration using standard curve (ranging from 0-10 μg/mL).
steps.reference: Chassaing et al., American Journal of Physiology. 2012

steps.title: Intestinal tissue harvest
steps.description: Following blood collection, euthanize mice by cervical dislocation under deep anesthesia. Excise entire small intestine from pylorus to cecum. Flush lumen with ice-cold PBS. Divide into three sections: proximal (duodenum), middle (jejunum), and distal (ileum). For each animal, allocate jejunum tissue as follows: 2cm segment in 10% neutral buffered formalin for IHC, 3cm segment snap-frozen in liquid nitrogen for Western blot, 2cm segment in RNAlater for qPCR.

steps.title: Tissue preservation
steps.description: Fix formalin samples for 24h at room temperature, then transfer to 70% ethanol until paraffin embedding. Store snap-frozen samples at -80°C. Store RNAlater samples at -20°C after overnight incubation at 4°C. Label all samples with unique identifiers.

steps.title: Protein extraction
steps.description: Homogenize 50mg frozen jejunum tissue in 500μL RIPA buffer (50mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 150mM NaCl, 1% NP-40, 0.5% sodium deoxycholate, 0.1% SDS) supplemented with protease inhibitor cocktail (1:100) and phosphatase inhibitors. Incubate on ice for 30 min with vortexing every 10 min. Centrifuge at 14,000×g for 15 min at 4°C. Collect supernatant and determine protein concentration using BCA assay.

steps.title: SDS-PAGE and immunoblotting
steps.description: Load 30μg protein per well on 10% SDS-PAGE gels. Run electrophoresis at 100V for 90 min. Transfer proteins to PVDF membranes at 100V for 60 min at 4°C. Block membranes with 5% non-fat milk in TBST for 1h at room temperature. Incubate with primary antibodies overnight at 4°C: rabbit anti-claudin-1 (1:1000), rabbit anti-occludin (1:1000), mouse anti-β-actin (1:5000). Wash 3×10 min with TBST. Incubate with HRP-conjugated secondary antibodies (1:5000) for 1h at room temperature. Wash 3×10 min. Develop using ECL substrate and image using ChemiDoc system.
steps.reference: Al-Sadi et al., American Journal of Pathology. 2008

steps.title: Densitometry analysis
steps.description: Quantify band intensity using ImageJ software. Normalize claudin-1 and occludin expression to β-actin loading control. Calculate fold change relative to control group mean. Analyze samples in duplicate.

steps.title: Tissue sectioning and deparaffinization
steps.description: Section paraffin-embedded jejunum tissue at 5μm thickness using microtome. Mount on positively charged slides. Deparaffinize in xylene (3×5 min) and rehydrate through graded ethanol series (100%, 95%, 70%, 50%, each 3 min) to distilled water.

steps.title: Antigen retrieval and immunostaining
steps.description: Perform heat-induced antigen retrieval in citrate buffer (10mM, pH 6.0) at 95°C for 20 min. Cool for 30 min. Block endogenous peroxidase with 3% H2O2 for 10 min. Block non-specific binding with 10% normal goat serum in PBS for 1h at room temperature. Incubate with primary antibodies (rabbit anti-claudin-1 at 1:200 or rabbit anti-occludin at 1:200) overnight at 4°C in humidified chamber. Wash 3×5 min with PBS. Incubate with biotinylated goat anti-rabbit secondary antibody (1:500) for 30 min. Apply ABC reagent for 30 min. Develop with DAB substrate for 3-5 min. Counterstain with hematoxylin for 1 min. Dehydrate and mount with Permount.
steps.reference: Anderson et al., Journal of Clinical Investigation. 2004

steps.title: Image acquisition and quantification
steps.description: Capture images at 400× magnification using brightfield microscope with digital camera. Analyze 10 random fields per section, 3 sections per animal. Quantify staining intensity using ImageJ color deconvolution plugin. Score intensity semi-quantitatively (0=negative, 1=weak, 2=moderate, 3=strong) and calculate H-score: Σ(intensity × percentage of positive area). Two blinded observers independently score all slides.

steps.title: Data analysis and interpretation
steps.description: Compile all data in GraphPad Prism 9. Test normality using Shapiro-Wilk test. Compare FITC-dextran concentrations, protein expression levels, and IHC scores between groups using unpaired t-test (if normal) or Mann-Whitney U test (if non-normal). Calculate percentage reduction in intestinal permeability as: [(Control mean - LGG mean)/Control mean] × 100. Perform correlation analysis between FITC-dextran levels and tight junction protein expression using Pearson or Spearman correlation. Present data as mean ± SEM. Consider p<0.05 statistically significant. Calculate statistical power post-hoc.
steps.reference: Cumming et al., Journal of Physiology. 2007

line_items.description: Purchase and housing of 40 C57BL/6 mice for 4 weeks
All chemicals, antibodies, culture media, and disposables
Fluorometer, microscope, Western blot system, microtome use

tasks: Prepare culture media and validate L. rhamnosus GG culture
Prepare fresh probiotic suspensions every 3 days
Paraffin embedding and sectioning of fixed tissues
IHC staining for claudin-1 and occludin

validation.primary_outcome: Serum FITC-dextran concentration at 4 hours post-gavage as a measure of intestinal permeability
validation.success_threshold: ≥30% reduction in FITC-dextran concentration in LGG group compared to control group (p<0.05)
validation.statistical_test: Unpaired t-test or Mann-Whitney U test depending on normality; Pearson/Spearman correlation for protein expression vs. permeability
validation.positive_result: LGG group shows ≥30% lower FITC-dextran levels with corresponding significant increases (≥1.3-fold) in claudin-1 and occludin protein expression by Western blot and IHC, with negative correlation between permeability and tight junction protein levels
validation.negative_result: No significant difference (<30% or p>0.05) in FITC-dextran levels between groups, or reduced permeability without corresponding tight junction protein upregulation, indicating alternative mechanisms or lack of effect
quality_controls: Daily verification of probiotic viability by CFU enumeration (should be 0.5-2.0 × 10^9 CFU per dose)
Western blot β-actin loading control shows equal loading across all samples (CV <15%)
Positive and negative control tissues for IHC validation
Inter-observer agreement for IHC scoring (Cohen's kappa >0.75)
Body weight change <10% during treatment period (indicator of treatment tolerance)
No bacterial contamination in control group tissues (confirmed by sterile tissue culture)
Technical replicates for Western blot with CV <20%
